title: Beneath King Charles’s Jokes and Decorum, Some Subtle Rebuttals to Trump
summary: King Charles III gently pushed back against President Trump’s attacks on Britain and NATO, and he spoke of the importance of checks and balances.

title: At State Dinner, King Charles Charms the Court of Trump
summary: King Charles III presented President Trump with a golden bell. “Should you ever need to get hold of us,” the king said, “well, just give us a ring!”

title: See the Menu for the British Royal State Dinner
summary: Spring-herbed ravioli and Dover sole meunière are among the dishes at the dinner honoring King Charles III of Britain and Queen Camilla.

title: Full Guest List for Trump’s State Dinner With Charles and Camilla
summary: Six members of the Supreme Court were invited to the dinner, alongside top administration officials, billionaires and Republican lawmakers.

title: DOJ Secures New James Comey Indictment Over Alleged Threat Against Trump
summary: The new case stems from a social media post showing seashells on a North Carolina beach that the Trump administration characterized as a threat against the president.

title: FCC Orders a Review of ABC’s Licenses Amid Feud Between Trump and Kimmel
summary: The agency said the review was related to the network’s diversity and inclusion policies. But it came amid a fight between the president and the network’s late night host, Jimmy Kimmel.

title: What to Know About the Kimmel vs. Trump Feud
summary: The president and first lady took exception to a joke. Jimmy Kimmel defended it.

title: OpenAI Trial Starts With Two Very Different Tales of a Company’s Early Years
summary: In the trial’s first day of testimony, Elon Musk said greed led co-founder Sam Altman to pull the A.I. lab away from its nonprofit roots. OpenAI says that’s nonsense.

title: Amid Iran War and Tensions with Neighbors, U.A.E. Goes Its Own Way
summary: The United Arab Emirates’ decision to leave OPEC has rocked the region, underscoring how the country, at odds with Saudi Arabia, is increasingly charting its own course.

title: Britain Summons Iran’s Ambassador Over Message to Iranians in U.K.
summary: Iran’s embassy in London posted a message on social media inviting Iranians living in Britain to register for a ‘Sacrifice for the Homeland’ campaign.

title: Gallows Humor Provides Respite From Mideast Wars
summary: Arab digital creators on social media are turning out edgy material to cope with the conflicts and bring levity to a pain that often feels too raw.

title: Trump’s Face Will Appear on Limited-Edition U.S. Passports, State Dept. Says
summary: The department said it planned to release “a limited number of specially designed” U.S. passports that feature a picture of the president to commemorate the country’s 250th anniversary in July.

title: Former Fauci Adviser Indicted on Covid-Related Charges
summary: Prosecutors accused Dr. David Morens, a former adviser to Dr. Anthony S. Fauci, of hiding records related to the onset of the pandemic.

title: White House Urges House to Quickly Fund D.H.S.
summary: The call amounted to a rebuke of Speaker Mike Johnson, who has delayed action on a homeland security spending bill and suggested this week that it needed changes.

title: Kennedy Center Works to Make the Case That Repairs Are Urgent
summary: In Washington and in federal court, the center is arguing that its planned two-year closure is crucial. Critics say it’s a result of declining attendance and fleeing artists.

title: Global Deforestation Slows, W.R.I. Report Finds. But Wildfires Are Taking a Toll.
summary: In 2025, the world razed less forest than any other year in the last decade. The bad news: global warming is making wildfires more frequent and intense.

title: Meta Charged With Failing to Keep Children Off Instagram and Facebook in Europe
summary: European Union regulators said the company did not have effective controls to check a user’s self-declared date of birth, in violation of an online safety law.

title: Trump Tests the Limits of His Most Faithful Supporters
summary: The president’s rhetoric on religion, along with hard-line immigration policies and the U.S. war in Iran, has splintered a coalition of Christian voters who returned him to the White House.

title: Takeaways From the California Governor Debate at Pomona College
summary: The candidates felt pressure to differentiate themselves, with ballots going out to voters next week. The result was a more spirited battle than past debates.

title: The War in Iran Has Americans Rethinking Their Summer Travel Plans
summary: Faced with geopolitical instability, economic uncertainty and rising airfares, many travelers are changing destinations or canceling their trips entirely.

title: Supreme Court Appears Skeptical of Falun Gong Lawsuit Against Cisco
summary: The court’s decision could have broader implications for lawsuits seeking to hold companies liable for international human rights abuses.

title: Offbeat Obituaries Celebrate Loss With Levity (and Brutal Honesty)
summary: Irreverent tributes filled with unvarnished truths and funny anecdotes, which run counter to more somber traditional obituaries, have gained attention for “how alive they feel,” a researcher said.

title: The Banality of Evil, Again
summary: The distinguishing feature of Cole Tomas Allen’s manifesto is its insipidity.

title: I Played Putin in a War Game. He Could Invade Again.
summary: NATO countries need to get their act together.

title: Jimmy Kimmel Spills the Tea on Trump’s Royal Meeting
summary: The late night show host called the king of England “one of the very few people who might actually be able to tip things even slightly in a positive direction.”

title: Dirk Kempthorne, Former Idaho Governor and Interior Secretary, Dies at 74
summary: A Republican, Mr. Kempthorne rose to prominence as the mayor of Boise in the late 1980s and early ’90s, as the city became a haven for outdoor enthusiasts.

title: Oakland’s Airport Can Use ‘San Francisco’ in Its Name Under Settlement
summary: The agreement ends a two-year dispute between the Bay Area cities over the smaller airport’s rebranding.

title: Mamdani’s Candidate Concedes in High-Profile Council Race in Manhattan
summary: The City Council special election pitting Lindsey Boylan, backed by Mayor Zohran Mamdani, against Carl Wilson, a community activist, was not officially called. But Ms. Boylan conceded.

title: ‘Dances With Wolves’ Actor Is Sentenced to Life in Prison
summary: Nathan Chasing Horse had been convicted of charges including the sexual assault of women and girls and the possession of child sexual abuse imagery.
